Pursuant to Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, the stale checks shall be marked “Cancelled” on their faces and reported as follows:
For spoiled checks which are immediately cancelled and for which the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) has not yet been prepared, the cancelled check shall be attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “Cancelled” shall be indicated in the report.
For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report.  The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
For checks which became spoil or stale in the hands of the payee and which require replacement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer.  A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check.  The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation.  The replacement check shall be reported chronologically in the RCI.

During the exit conference the Municipal Accountant agreed to adhere with our recommendation. He also committed to submit the composition of other  Current Liability account not later than April 30, 2018 and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the non-current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic account.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal  Treasurer to transfer the funds to the General Fund of the LGU and also for the Municipal Accountant to apply the same in its book to comply with the provisions of RA 7160 and for proper presentation of LGU fund balances in the Financial Statements.
Management committed to follow the audit team’s recommendation.

We recommended the management for the timely submission of accounts pursuant to COA Circulars 95-006 and 2009-06 and as required by PD 1445.
Management committed to adhere with the recommendation of the audit team.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Vice-Mayor to refund the cost of plane tickets amounting to P 11,361.00. It is also recommended to refrain from using other means of transportation other than those authorized and ensure the economy and efficiency is observed.
During the exit conference management explained that the travel of the Municipal Vice-Mayor thru airplane is necessary due to the limited time in order to catch up with his scheduled meetings/appointments. On the other hand, management agreed to refund travelling expenses claimed by the drivers of the subject-officials

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to refund the Transportation Allowance totaling to P50,400.00. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to discontinue the grant of Transportation Allowance  to the Municipal Mayor and ensure the compliance of the agency with NBC No. 548.
During the exit conference, management agreed that the payment of Transportation Allowance to the Local Chief Executive shall be discontinued, however, the concerned official pleaded for the prospective application of this observation.

We recommended the management the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to submit immediately the required documents as embodied in Section 5.15 of COA Circular 2012-01 for our review.
Submit the duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket and Fuel Consumption Report pertaining to the road rehabilitation project for our review and verification.
Submit the lacking documents as presented in Annex B and ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements as enumerated in Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular 2012-01 to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances.
Submit lacking documents as required by Annex “H” Appendix A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 for the LGU’s transactions involving alternative modes of procurements presented in 3.1.d.
Require the contractors to submit construction and health program approved by the DOLE in compliance to the requirement of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended the management to refrain from approving transaction without proper attachment of documentary requirements.
The needed documents to support the granting of loyalty pay have been submitted by the management, however, other documents required by the audit team were still being collated by management and committed to submit the same not later than April 30, 2018. Management also agreed to require the contractor to submit DOLE approved CHSP.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Engineer together with the inspection committee for infrastructure projects to thoroughly inspect the implementation and completion of projects of the LGU and ensure the compliance of the contractor with the program of works and change order, if any, to avoid further disallowances.
The deficiency is already settled by the contractor thru Official Receipt No. 9294393 dated March 23, 2018 amounting to P8,918.53.

We recommended the management to require the submission of warranty securities in the form of a retention money or bank guarantee for transactions with unexpired warranty period and for future procurement to prevent any government loss and/or audit suspensions.
Management is in the process of collecting warranty security from contractors with goods delivered with unexpired warranty period and submit the same not later than May 15, 2018.

We recommended the Municipal Engineer to submit the basis for the computation of fuel and lubricant consumption as presented in the POW in order to establish the reliability of the figures disclosed. We also recommended the Municipal Engineer to prepare LGU POW’s in standard form.
During the exit conference management instructed the Municipal Engineer to submit a more detailed Program of Works for the project not later than May 15, 2018.

We recommended the management to carefully plan its procurement process taking into consideration the availability of funds and accessibility of project site. As the implementing agency, the LGU is expected to initiate in coordinating with the source agency for the early release of funds in order not to affect the timely delivery of service and benefits to the public.
Management committed to adhere with the audit team’s recommendation.

We recommended the management to promptly implement PPA’s finance by subject-fund to ensure that socio-economic benefits expected to produce by these projects will be enjoyed by constituents within the budget year and ensure continuous economic development in the community. We also recommended to submit the status of the remaining projects which were not reported in the quarterly PPA as presented in Annex D.
Management committed for prompt implementation of PPA’s funded by the 20% development fund. Likewise, management already submitted the quarterly report on PPA by the LGU as of March 31, 2018.

We recommended the management to ensure that the benefits are delivered to the beneficiaries in an effective and efficient manner by carefully reviewing the PPA’s gender-responsiveness before incorporating it in the Annual GPB of the LGU, full utilization of the GAD Fund in accordance with enacted and DILG-Approved GPB and monitor strict implementation of the PPA’s.
During the exit conference, management agreed to monitor the full and proper implementation/utilization of the GAD Fund.

Stop the procurement of goods on reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application;
Require the department head to submit the Project  Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of their offices for consolidation into an Annual Procurement Plan (APP) by the Bids and Awards Committee for proper planning and execution of the procurement process so as for them not to resort to reimbursement basis.
